name: My Chicken Almond Stir Fry
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the chicken, bok choy or napa cabbage, water chestnuts, snow peas, bamboo shoots, celery, and almonds as per desired quantities.
2. Heat a wok over high heat until a drop of water sizzles into steam.
3. Add about 1 tablespoon vegetable oil to the hot wok and swirl around.
4. Stir-fry the blanched almonds for about 2 minutes or until just golden brown, then remove them with a slotted spoon and set aside.
5. Reheat the remaining oil in the wok and add ginger (sliced) and garlic cloves (smashed). Stir-fry for 30 seconds, then discard the ginger and garlic.
6. Add the diced chicken to the hot wok and stir-fry for 2 minutes until cooked through.
7. Toss in the mixed vegetables (bok choy or napa cabbage, water chestnuts, snow peas, bamboo shoots, and celery) and cook for another 30 seconds to coat with oil.
8. Add the soy sauce mixture (made by combining half the chicken stock with soy sauce, sugar, and pepper) to the wok and stir-fry briefly.
9. Cover the wok and steam the ingredients for 2 minutes or until the vegetables are tender but crisp and the chicken is fully cooked.
10. Stir in the corn starch mixture (remaining chicken stock mixed with corn starch) and cook uncovered for an additional 30 seconds to glaze the dish.
11. Transfer the stir-fry to a heated platter, garnished with almonds, and serve over cooked rice.
(Note: Rice can be prepared by cooking 2 cups of water and 1 cup of rice with 1½ teaspoons salt in a microwave-safe dish for about 18 minutes.)
